_meta.purpose: Authoritative GPS coordinates for the zones referenced by the LOVS pipeline output, plus reference geographic features for the brief's geographic map.
verification_notes: Town coordinates verified against Wikipedia / OpenStreetMap reference articles; cross-checked with GeoNames when ambiguous.
DRC health zones are referenced by their administrative seat (town) coordinates, which is the reporting locus.
Uganda districts are referenced by their administrative HQ town coordinates.

Border line and lake outlines are simplified approximations (10-15 vertices each) traced from Natural Earth 1:10m public domain data. Suitable for a regional context map at the brief's scale; not suitable for cadastral or boundary-claim work.

Model-abstracted zone names that do not map to a verified geographic referent are recorded with `geographic_referent: null` and excluded from the projected map. Their case counts are still reflected in the snapshot totals.

Live re-verification 2026-05-20: Wikipedia infobox values cross-checked for all 13 zones. Two coordinates updated: Kinshasa (transcription correction from -4.4419 -> -4.3219, source citation already correct) and Mongbwalu (precision upgrade from 1.9831 to 1.9352 to match the current infobox). All other zone coordinates are within 0.02 degrees (~2 km) of the current Wikipedia infobox, consistent with the historical DMS source citations stored in each zone.

zones.name: Aru Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Aru Health Zone, Aru Territory, Ituri Province. INRB-UMIE INSP per-zone tables surface this zone at as_of 2026-05-26. Polygon centroid from the RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile row Nom=Aru, zscode=CD5409ZS04.
zones.source: Promoted under Plan A 2026-05-28 threshold-based source-zone criterion (THRESHOLD_SUSPECTED_LOW=4). Coordinates from RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile, row Nom=Aru, PROVINCE=Ituri, zscode=CD5409ZS04; verified against the INRB-UMIE consortium release at github.com/INRB-UMIE/Ebola_DRC_2026 build-2026-05-27-e40bc9e drc_health_zones.geojson.

zones.name: Arua
zones.geographic_referent: Arua (Uganda), the largest city in northwestern Uganda, adjacent to Rhino Camp refugee settlement (~95k refugees per UNHCR late-2025). Connected to Mahagi/Goli via the Northern Corridor. Not a confirmed-case zone; it is a cross-border corridor target in the current pinned calibration set (bunia and rwampara to arua-uga) on the NW Uganda axis from the Mahagi/Goli crossing.
zones.source: Wikipedia: Arua (3°01'13"N 30°54'38"E). Refugee settlement population: UNHCR Uganda Refugee Operations.

zones.name: Aungba
zones.geographic_referent: Aungba Health Zone, Djugu Territory, Ituri Province (DRC)
zones.source: GRID3 COD Health Zones v8.0 polygon centroid (interior representative point). INRB SitRep #015 (2026-05-29) named affected; seat-town/DHIS2 confirmation pending before any public release.

zones.name: Bambu Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Bambu Health Zone, Djugu Territory, Ituri Province. Centroid computed from the public RGC.cd 2019 DRC health-zone shapefile polygon row Nom=Bambu, PROVINCE=Ituri, TERRITOIRE=Djugu, Pcode=CD5405ZS01, Code_DHIS2=Uc3GqWS8C0w.
zones.source: DRC MoH SitRep MVE N 007/MVB_17/2026 cumulative Table IV names this health zone as confirmed-case affected. Coordinates: polygon centroid computed from Referentiel Geographique Commun (RGC.cd), 'zone_sante190613.zip' / zone_sante.shp, published as a 519-zone DRC health-zone shapefile, last update 2019-06-07 and validation 2019-06-13; downloaded from https://rgc.cd/images/RGCdata/zone_sante190613.zip, SHA-256 e0b672d7ad6f387c26d13ad357185d1152139b91fb7ba70235def55888db9702. RGC row reliability field is blank.

zones.name: Beni
zones.geographic_referent: Beni (capital of Beni Territory, North Kivu Province)
zones.source: Wikipedia: Beni, Democratic Republic of the Congo (0°29'29"N 29°28'19"E)

zones.name: Bundibugyo District
zones.geographic_referent: Bundibugyo District (administrative HQ town: Bundibugyo)
zones.source: Wikipedia: Bundibugyo District (0°42'N 30°04'E for the district HQ town)

zones.name: Bunia
zones.geographic_referent: Bunia (capital of Ituri Province)
zones.source: Wikipedia: Bunia (1°33'44"N 30°15'35"E)

zones.name: Butembo
zones.geographic_referent: Butembo city, used as the public coordinate proxy for Butembo Health Zone until a vetted polygon centroid is added.
zones.source: WHO AFRO SitRep-01 (data as of 18 May 2026) names Butembo HZ with 1 confirmed case. Coordinates: Geodatos Butembo WGS84 decimal coordinate, 0.14164°N 29.29117°E, cross-checkable against public gazetteer/Wikipedia city references.

zones.name: Damas Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Damas Health Zone, Djugu Territory, Ituri Province. INRB-UMIE INSP per-zone tables surface this zone at as_of 2026-05-26. Polygon centroid from the RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile row Nom=Damas, zscode=CD5405ZS02.
zones.source: Promoted under Plan A 2026-05-28 threshold-based source-zone criterion. Coordinates from RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile, row Nom=Damas, PROVINCE=Ituri, TERRITOIRE=Djugu, zscode=CD5405ZS02; cross-verified against the INRB-UMIE consortium release drc_health_zones.geojson.

zones.name: Gety
zones.geographic_referent: Gety Health Zone, Irumu Territory, Ituri Province (DRC)
zones.source: GRID3 COD Health Zones v8.0 polygon centroid (interior representative point). INRB SitRep #015 (2026-05-29) named affected; seat-town/DHIS2 confirmation pending before any public release.

zones.name: Goma
zones.geographic_referent: Goma (capital of North Kivu Province, on the Rwanda border). One laboratory-confirmed BDBV case on 17 May 2026, a woman who travelled from Ituri Province.
zones.source: Wikipedia: Goma (1°40'45"S 29°13'22"E). Case figure: Wikipedia '2026 Ituri Province Ebola epidemic' citing primary news outlets for 17 May 2026.

zones.name: Ituri Province
zones.geographic_referent: Province-level label, not a specific point. The three affected health zones (Mongbwalu, Rwampara, Bunia) sit inside this province. The corridor model treats 'ituri' as a candidate source label; this is a known model artifact (province-level rollup) rather than a verified third source zone.
zones.source: Pipeline province-level label; the WHO and Africa CDC sources name HZs, not the province, as the case-attribution geography.

zones.name: Kalunguta
zones.geographic_referent: Kalunguta Health Zone, Beni Territory area, Nord-Kivu Province (DRC)
zones.source: GRID3 COD Health Zones v8.0 polygon centroid (interior representative point). INRB SitRep #015 (2026-05-29) named affected; seat-town/DHIS2 confirmation pending before any public release.

zones.name: Kampala
zones.geographic_referent: Kampala (capital of Uganda). 2 confirmed cases including 1 death per WHO PHEIC statement (17 May 2026) and Africa CDC PHECS declaration (18 May 2026).
zones.source: Wikipedia: Kampala (0°18'58.8"N 32°34'56.4"E)

zones.name: Karisimbi Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Karisimbi Health Zone, Goma City, North Kivu Province. Sibling HZ to goma-cod within the Goma urban agglomeration (centroid spacing 4.9 km, both share zscode prefix CD6101 for Goma Health Area 1 in the RGC.cd shapefile). Polygon centroid from the RGC.cd row Nom=Karisimbi, zscode=CD6101ZS02. Treated as a distinct LOVS source zone with independent INSP attribution; downstream consumers group sibling-HZ entries visually per spec §6.9.
zones.source: Promoted under Plan A 2026-05-28 threshold-based source-zone criterion. Coordinates from RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile, row Nom=Karisimbi, PROVINCE=North Kivu, zscode=CD6101ZS02; cross-verified against the INRB-UMIE consortium release drc_health_zones.geojson. Sibling-HZ cluster metadata per Plan A v2 sibling-HZ doctrine (spec §6.9, precedent: Bunia/Nizi/Rwampara cluster in central Ituri at 12-14 km centroid spacing).

zones.name: Kasese District
zones.geographic_referent: Kasese District (administrative HQ town: Kasese)
zones.source: Wikipedia: Kasese (0°11'N 30°05'E)

zones.name: Katwa
zones.geographic_referent: Katwa locality near Butembo, used as the public coordinate proxy for Katwa Health Zone until a vetted polygon centroid is added.
zones.source: WHO AFRO SitRep-01 (data as of 18 May 2026) names Katwa HZ with 1 confirmed case. Coordinates: Nona.net Katwa place page reports 0°06'00"S 29°19'00"E, converted to WGS84 decimal -0.1000, 29.3167.

zones.name: Kilo Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Kilo Health Zone, Djugu Territory, Ituri Province. The DRC MoH PDF labels the affected row as Kilo Mission; the RGC health-zone polygon row is Nom=Kilo, PROVINCE=Ituri, TERRITOIRE=Djugu, Pcode=CD5405ZS06, Code_DHIS2=sbyWSsdZ3tj.
zones.source: DRC MoH SitRep MVE N 007/MVB_17/2026 cumulative Table IV names this health zone as confirmed-case affected. Coordinates: polygon centroid computed from Referentiel Geographique Commun (RGC.cd), 'zone_sante190613.zip' / zone_sante.shp, published as a 519-zone DRC health-zone shapefile, last update 2019-06-07 and validation 2019-06-13; downloaded from https://rgc.cd/images/RGCdata/zone_sante190613.zip, SHA-256 e0b672d7ad6f387c26d13ad357185d1152139b91fb7ba70235def55888db9702. RGC row reliability field is blank.

zones.name: Kinshasa
zones.geographic_referent: Kinshasa (capital of DRC). A case reported on 16 May 2026 was later negative for Bundibugyo virus on confirmatory INRB testing per WHO, and is therefore not counted as confirmed. Outside the map viewport (lon 15.31°E lies west of the brief's eastern-DRC bounding box); retained only for auditability of the deconfirmed report.
zones.source: Wikipedia: Kinshasa (04°19'19"S 15°18'43"E / 4.32194°S 15.31194°E), live infobox value retrieved 2026-05-20. Case-status correction: WHO PHEIC statement / WHO DON602 update says the reported Kinshasa case tested negative on confirmatory INRB testing.

zones.name: Komanda Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Komanda Health Zone, Irumu Territory, Ituri Province. INRB-UMIE INSP per-zone tables surface this zone at as_of 2026-05-26 with 1 confirmed_death and 0 confirmed/suspected cases (the real instance of the `mixed_with_metric_floor` scale-resilience case per spec §6.7). Polygon centroid from the RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile row Nom=Komanda, zscode=CD5402ZS04.
zones.source: Promoted under Plan A 2026-05-28 threshold-based source-zone criterion (criterion satisfied via THRESHOLD_CONFIRMED_DEATHS=1). Coordinates from RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile, row Nom=Komanda, PROVINCE=Ituri, TERRITOIRE=Irumu, zscode=CD5402ZS04; cross-verified against the INRB-UMIE consortium release drc_health_zones.geojson.

zones.name: Kyondo
zones.geographic_referent: Kyondo Health Zone, Lubero Territory, Nord-Kivu Province (DRC)
zones.source: GRID3 COD Health Zones v8.0 polygon centroid (interior representative point). INRB SitRep #015 (2026-05-29) named affected; seat-town/DHIS2 confirmation pending before any public release.

zones.name: Lita
zones.geographic_referent: Lita Health Zone, Djugu Territory, Ituri Province (DRC)
zones.source: GRID3 COD Health Zones v8.0 polygon centroid (interior representative point). INRB SitRep #015 (2026-05-29) named affected; seat-town/DHIS2 confirmation pending before any public release.

zones.name: Mahagi
zones.role: border-crossing watch (not in current corridor calibration set)
zones.geographic_referent: Mahagi (DRC), on the Goli-Mahagi-Bunia road and one of three priority cross-border roads jointly named by Uganda and DRC. The Mahagi / Goli pair forms one of DRC's busiest border crossings on the East African Northern Corridor. NOT a confirmed-case zone as of the snapshot date; included on the map for orientation because the current corridor calibration set does NOT include the Mahagi-Arua corridor (see Blindspots).
zones.source: Wikipedia: Mahagi (2°18'48"N 30°59'03"E). Cross-border infrastructure: Government of Uganda + Government of DRC + TradeMark Africa Mahagi OSBP documentation 2023-2025.

zones.name: Mambasa Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Mambasa Health Zone, Mambasa Territory, Ituri Province. INRB-UMIE INSP per-zone tables surface this zone at as_of 2026-05-26. Polygon centroid from the RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile row Nom=Mambasa, zscode=CD5403ZS02.
zones.source: Promoted under Plan A 2026-05-28 threshold-based source-zone criterion. Coordinates from RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile, row Nom=Mambasa, PROVINCE=Ituri, TERRITOIRE=Mambasa, zscode=CD5403ZS02; cross-verified against the INRB-UMIE consortium release drc_health_zones.geojson.

zones.name: Mangala
zones.geographic_referent: Mangala Health Zone, Ituri Province (DRC)
zones.source: GRID3 COD Health Zones v8.0 polygon centroid (interior representative point). INRB SitRep #015 (2026-05-29) named affected; seat-town/DHIS2 confirmation pending before any public release.

zones.name: Miti-Murhesa Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Miti-Murhesa Health Zone, Kabare Territory, South Kivu. DRC MoH SitRep MVE N 007/MVB_17/2026 cumulative Table IV names Miti Murhesa with one confirmed case as of 2026-05-21. This coordinate is the public CRSN-Lwiro locality proxy, not a vetted health-zone polygon centroid or case-site GPS point.
zones.source: DRC MoH SitRep MVE N 007/MVB_17/2026 cumulative Table IV names Miti Murhesa in Sud-Kivu. Coordinate proxy: public CRSN-Lwiro coordinate 2.23955 S, 28.81237 E; Radio Okapi (21 May 2026) and 7sur7 (22 May 2026) provide local context around Miti-Murhesa/Kabare/Lwiro but are not the source of the model count.

zones.name: Mongbwalu
zones.geographic_referent: Mongbwalu (Djugu Territory, Ituri Province)
zones.source: Wikipedia: Mongbwalu (1°56'07"N 30°02'46"E / 1.935157°N 30.046234°E), live infobox value retrieved 2026-05-20

zones.name: Nebbi
zones.geographic_referent: Nebbi (Uganda), district HQ town in the West Nile sub-region, about 80km southeast of Arua on the Pakwach-Nebbi-Arua road; same NW Uganda cross-border axis as Arua relative to the Mahagi/Goli crossing. A cross-border corridor target in the current pinned calibration set (mongbwalu to nebbi-uga).

zones.name: Nizi Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Nizi Health Zone, Djugu Territory, Ituri Province. Centroid computed from the public RGC.cd 2019 DRC health-zone shapefile polygon row Nom=Nizi, PROVINCE=Iturie, TERRITOIRE=Djugu, Pcode=CD5405ZS11, Code_DHIS2=TCE5waEW7Fi. The RGC row reliability field is Basse.
zones.source: DRC MoH SitRep MVE N 007/MVB_17/2026 cumulative Table IV names this health zone as confirmed-case affected. Coordinates: polygon centroid computed from Referentiel Geographique Commun (RGC.cd), 'zone_sante190613.zip' / zone_sante.shp, published as a 519-zone DRC health-zone shapefile, last update 2019-06-07 and validation 2019-06-13; downloaded from https://rgc.cd/images/RGCdata/zone_sante190613.zip, SHA-256 e0b672d7ad6f387c26d13ad357185d1152139b91fb7ba70235def55888db9702. RGC row reliability: Basse.

zones.name: Nyankunde
zones.geographic_referent: Nyankunde hospital/reference locality in Irumu, used as the public coordinate proxy for Nyankunde Health Zone until a vetted polygon centroid is added.
zones.source: WHO AFRO SitRep-01 (data as of 18 May 2026) names Nyankunde HZ with 4 confirmed cases. Coordinates: Mapcarta/OpenStreetMap Nyankunde hospital page reports 1.43152°N 30.03284°E.

zones.name: Oicha Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Oicha Health Zone, Beni Territory, North Kivu Province. INRB-UMIE INSP per-zone tables surface this zone at as_of 2026-05-26. Polygon centroid from the RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile row Nom=Oicha, zscode=CD6107ZS06.
zones.source: Promoted under Plan A 2026-05-28 threshold-based source-zone criterion. Coordinates from RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile, row Nom=Oicha, PROVINCE=North Kivu, TERRITOIRE=Beni, zscode=CD6107ZS06; cross-verified against the INRB-UMIE consortium release drc_health_zones.geojson.

zones.name: Rimba Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Rimba Health Zone, Mahagi Territory, Ituri Province. INRB-UMIE INSP per-zone tables surface this zone at as_of 2026-05-26. Polygon centroid from the RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile row Nom=Rimba, zscode=CD5407ZS07.
zones.source: Promoted under Plan A 2026-05-28 threshold-based source-zone criterion. Coordinates from RGC.cd zone_sante shapefile, row Nom=Rimba, PROVINCE=Ituri, TERRITOIRE=Mahagi, zscode=CD5407ZS07; cross-verified against the INRB-UMIE consortium release drc_health_zones.geojson.

zones.name: Rwampara Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Rwampara Health Zone (Irumu Territory, Ituri Province). Centroid computed from the public RGC.cd 2019 DRC health-zone shapefile polygon row Nom=Rwampara, PROVINCE=ITURI, TERRITOIRE=Irumu, Pcode=CD5402ZS06, Code_DHIS2=vPxN2nZnBXK. The RGC row's Fiabilite field is Basse, so this is a public boundary centroid suitable for map placement, not a field-verified facility point.
zones.source: WHO DON 602 (15 May 2026) names Rwampara HZ verbatim. Coordinates: polygon centroid computed from Référentiel Géographique Commun (RGC.cd), 'zone_sante190613.zip' / zone_sante.shp, published as a 519-zone DRC health-zone shapefile, last update 2019-06-07 and validation 2019-06-13; downloaded from https://rgc.cd/images/RGCdata/zone_sante190613.zip, SHA-256 e0b672d7ad6f387c26d13ad357185d1152139b91fb7ba70235def55888db9702. RGC row reliability: Basse.

zones.name: Logo Health Zone
zones.geographic_referent: Logo Health Zone, Mahagi territory, Ituri Province. SitRep #019 Table 1 reports 1 confirmed case in Logo. Centroid approximated by the Logo locality (OpenStreetMap: 'Logo, Ituri', village; ~15 km SW of Mahagi).
zones.source: OpenStreetMap (village 'Logo, Ituri'); Logo HZ center approximated by the locality.

drc_uganda_border.source: Simplified hand-trace from Natural Earth 1:10m admin_0_boundary_lines_land. ~18 vertices covering the DRC-Uganda border within the bounding box. Cross-checked against Wikipedia town coordinates so Bundibugyo (UGA, 30.062°E) and Beni (COD, 29.472°E) lie on their correct sides. Not for boundary-claim work.

lake_albert.source: Simplified Natural Earth 1:10m lakes layer. Shared between DRC (west shore) and Uganda (east shore); the border passes through the lake. Approximate visual outline.

lake_edward.source: Simplified Natural Earth 1:10m lakes layer. Shared between DRC and Uganda. Approximate visual outline.

bounding_box.comment: Covers Ituri + North Kivu + the South Kivu Miti-Murhesa review locality (DRC east, including Goma) and Western, Northern, and Central Uganda (including Arua up north). Excludes Kinshasa (1500 km west), retained only as a deconfirmed-case audit entry and not plotted.
